 {"id":"gt-9a2.7","title":"CloudRun protocol types: WorkRequest, WorkEvent, WorkResult","description":"## Overview

HTTP protocol types for Cloud Run work dispatch. **Types only** - server and client are separate tasks.

## Request Type

```go
type WorkRequest struct {
    IssueID string          `json:"issue_id"`
    Rig     RigConfig       `json:"rig"`
    Beads   BeadsConfig     `json:"beads"`
    Branch  string          `json:"worker_branch"`
    Context map[string]any  `json:"context,omitempty"`
}

type RigConfig struct {
    URL    string `json:"url"`
    Branch string `json:"branch"`
}

type BeadsConfig struct {
    URL    string `json:"url"`
    Branch string `json:"branch"`
}
```

## Response Types (streaming NDJSON)

```go
type WorkEvent struct {
    Type     string `json:"type"`  // status, log, progress, result, error
    Status   string `json:"status,omitempty"`
    Line     string `json:"line,omitempty"`
    Percent  int    `json:"percent,omitempty"`
    Branch   string `json:"branch,omitempty"`
    PRURL    string `json:"pr_url,omitempty"`
    Code     string `json:"code,omitempty"`
    Message  string `json:"message,omitempty"`
}

type WorkResult struct {
    Status  string `json:"status"`  // done, failed
    Branch  string `json:"branch"`
    PRURL   string `json:"pr_url,omitempty"`
    Error   string `json:"error,omitempty"`
}
```

## Files

- `internal/cloudrun/protocol.go` - All types above

## Notes

This is just types. Server (gt-9a2.7b) and client (gt-9a2.7c) are separate tasks.
Small, focused task - can complete quickly.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T18:02:51.480019-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T18:14:49.196218-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-9a2.7","depends_on_id":"gt-9a2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-16T18:02:51.48197-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-9a2.7","depends_on_id":"gt-9a2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T18:03:45.984572-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-9a2.8","title":"CloudRunOutpost: Basic implementation","description":"## Overview

Basic CloudRunOutpost implementation. Persistent connections and cost tracking are separate tasks.

## Implementation

```go
type CloudRunOutpost struct {
    name        string
    project     string
    region      string
    service     string
    maxWorkers  int
    client      *WorkClient
    workers     map[string]*CloudRunWorker
    mu          sync.RWMutex
}

func NewCloudRunOutpost(cfg OutpostConfig) (*CloudRunOutpost, error) {
    serviceURL := fmt.Sprintf(
        "https://%s-%s.a.run.app",
        cfg.Service, cfg.Region,
    )
    return &CloudRunOutpost{
        name:       cfg.Name,
        project:    cfg.Project,
        region:     cfg.Region,
        service:    cfg.Service,
        maxWorkers: cfg.MaxWorkers,
        client:     NewWorkClient(serviceURL),
        workers:    make(map[string]*CloudRunWorker),
    }, nil
}
```

## Spawn

```go
func (o *CloudRunOutpost) Spawn(issue string, cfg WorkerConfig) (Worker, error) {
    req := WorkRequest{
        IssueID: issue,
        Rig:     RigConfig{URL: cfg.RigURL, Branch: cfg.GitBranch},
        Beads:   BeadsConfig{URL: cfg.BeadsURL, Branch: "beads-sync"},
        Branch:  "polecat/" + issue,
    }
    
    events, err := o.client.DispatchWork(context.Background(), req)
    if err != nil {
        return nil, err
    }
    
    worker := &CloudRunWorker{
        id:      uuid.New().String(),
        outpost: o.name,
        issue:   issue,
        events:  events,
        status:  WorkerStatusWorking,
    }
    
    o.mu.Lock()
    o.workers[worker.id] = worker
    o.mu.Unlock()
    
    go worker.monitor()
    return worker, nil
}
```

## CloudRunWorker

```go
type CloudRunWorker struct {
    id      string
    outpost string
    issue   string
    status  WorkerStatus
    events  <-chan WorkEvent
    logs    []string
}

func (w *CloudRunWorker) Attach() error {
    return errors.New("Cloud Run workers do not support attach")
}

func (w *CloudRunWorker) Logs() (io.Reader, error) {
    return strings.NewReader(strings.Join(w.logs, "\n")), nil
}
```

## Files

- `internal/outpost/cloudrun.go`

## Dependencies

Depends on: gt-9a2.1 (interfaces), gt-9a2.12 (HTTP client)

 {"id":"gt-9a2.9","title":"Outpost assignment policy: Smart work routing","description":"## Overview

Policy engine for deciding which outpost gets which work.

## Policy Configuration

```yaml
policy:
  # Default order of preference
  default_preference: [local, gce-burst, cloudrun-burst]
  
  # Rules applied in order
  rules:
    # Background work → Cloud Run (cheap)
    - condition: "priority >= P3"
      prefer: cloudrun-burst
      
    # Long tasks → VM (persistent)
    - condition: "estimated_duration > 30m"
      prefer: gce-burst
      
    # Specific epic → specific outpost
    - condition: "epic == gt-abc"
      prefer: local
```

## Implementation

```go
type AssignmentPolicy struct {
    DefaultPreference []string
    Rules             []PolicyRule
}

type PolicyRule struct {
    Condition string   // Simple expression
    Prefer    string   // Outpost name
    Require   string   // Must use this outpost
}

func (p *AssignmentPolicy) SelectOutpost(
    issue Issue, 
    outposts map[string]Outpost,
) Outpost {
    // Check rules in order
    for _, rule := range p.Rules {
        if rule.Matches(issue) {
            if op, ok := outposts[rule.Prefer]; ok {
                if op.ActiveWorkers() < op.MaxWorkers() {
                    return op
                }
            }
        }
    }
    
    // Fall back to default preference
    for _, name := range p.DefaultPreference {
        if op, ok := outposts[name]; ok {
            if op.ActiveWorkers() < op.MaxWorkers() {
                return op
            }
        }
    }
    
    return nil  // All outposts at capacity
}
```

## Condition Language

Simple expressions, not a full DSL:

```
priority >= P3
priority == P0
estimated_duration > 30m
epic == gt-abc
type == bug
label contains "urgent"
```

## Files

- `internal/outpost/policy.go`
- `internal/outpost/condition.go`

-{"id":"gt-9g82","title":"Polecat wisp architecture: Proto → Wisp → Mol pattern","description":"Design the three-layer architecture for polecat lifecycle:

## The Insight

Polecats should run a ONE-SHOT WISP (not looping like patrols):

**Step 1: Onboard**
- Read full polecat protocol (polecat.md template)
- Learn Gas Town operation, exit strategies, molecule protocol
- This is the 'how to be a polecat' education

**Step 2: Execute Mol**
- Run the assigned molecule (the actual work item)
- Could span multiple sessions via session continuity
- The mol is pure work content (epic, issue, feature)

**Step 3: Cleanup**
- Run final step of the wisp
- Self-delete / request shutdown

## Three Layers

- **Proto**: polecat.md template (instructions for being a polecat)
- **Wisp**: One-shot harness instantiated from proto (wraps the mol)
- **Mol**: The work item (issue/epic being processed)

## Why This Matters

1. **Separation of concerns**: Protocol (how) vs Work (what)
2. **Reusability**: Same wisp harness wraps any mol
3. **Extensibility**: Plugin points for custom behavior
4. **Session continuity**: Wisp handles multi-session, not the mol
5. **Blurred control/data planes**: Intentional in Gas Town

## Design Questions

1. How does proto (polecat.md) become a wisp instance?
2. What are the plugin/extension points?
3. Should all 'engineer in a box' mols use proto → wisp → mol?
4. How does this relate to refinery/deacon patterns?","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-22T15:54:17.446941-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T15:54:38.352563-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-9j9","title":"CLI: worker status reporting commands","description":"Worker status reporting CLI for polecats to report progress.

## Commands

### gt worker started
```
gt worker started <issue-id> [-m MESSAGE]
```
Reports work started on issue.

### gt worker progress
```
gt worker progress <issue-id> <0-100> [-m MESSAGE]
```
Reports percentage complete.

### gt worker blocked
```
gt worker blocked <issue-id> <reason> [-m MESSAGE]
```
Reports blocked status with reason.

### gt worker completed
```
gt worker completed <issue-id> [-m MESSAGE]
```
Reports task completion.

### gt worker failed
```
gt worker failed <issue-id> <reason> [-m MESSAGE]
```
Reports task failure.

## Implementation
Each command sends mail to refinery with structured content:
```go
type WorkerStatusReport struct {
    IssueID    string
    Status     string  // started|progress|blocked|completed|failed
    Progress   int     // 0-100 for progress
    Reason     string  // for blocked/failed
    Message    string  // optional detail
    ReportedAt time.Time
}
```

## Message Format
Subject: "[STATUS] <issue-id>: <status>"
Body: JSON-encoded WorkerStatusReport

## Default Recipient
```go
// Determine from context
func getDefaultRecipient() string {
    rig := os.Getenv("GT_RIG")
    if rig != "" {
        return rig + "/refinery"
    }
    return "refinery/"
}
```

## New File
internal/cmd/worker.go

## PGT Reference
gastown-py/src/gastown/cli/worker_cmd.py

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 5 commands implemented
- [ ] Status sent as mail to refinery
- [ ] Structured JSON body for parsing
- [ ] Works from polecat session context","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T14:47:52.795695-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T16:05:26.715967-08:00"}

-{"id":"gt-drbd","title":"Add no-PR instructions to mol-polecat-work at two points","description":"Update mol-polecat-work in builtin_molecules.go to explicitly forbid GitHub PRs.

## Two Points to Add Instructions

### 1. submit-work step
When polecat is ready to submit:
- Push branch to origin
- Create beads merge-request issue
- DO NOT use gh pr create or GitHub PRs

### 2. CLAUDE.md polecat context
Add to polecat role instructions:
- Never use gh pr create
- Never create GitHub pull requests
- The Refinery processes merges via beads MR issues

## Why Two Points
- Molecule step description guides the workflow
- CLAUDE.md reinforces at context level
- Belt and suspenders approach

## Implementation
1. Update PolecatWorkMolecule() submit-work step description
2. Update prompts/roles/polecat.md with explicit prohibition

 {"id":"gt-gby","title":"gt handoff: Unified agent lifecycle command","description":"## Summary

Unified `gt handoff` command for ALL agent types to request lifecycle actions.

## Usage

gt handoff              # Context-aware default
gt handoff --shutdown   # Terminate, cleanup, don't restart
gt handoff --cycle      # Restart with handoff mail
gt handoff --restart    # Fresh restart, no handoff

## Context-Aware Defaults

| Agent Type | Default | Reason |
|------------|---------|--------|
| Polecat | --shutdown | Ephemeral, work is done |
| Witness | --cycle | Long-running, context full |
| Refinery | --cycle | Long-running, context full |
| Mayor | --cycle | Long-running, context full |
| Crew | (sends mail only) | Human-managed |

## What gt handoff Does

1. **Verify safe to stop**
   - Git state clean (no uncommitted changes)
   - Work handed off (PR exists for polecats)

2. **Send handoff mail to self** (for cycle/restart)
   - Captures current state
   - New session will read this

3. **Send lifecycle request to manager**
   - Polecats/Refinery → Witness
   - Witness/Mayor → Daemon
   - Format: mail to <manager> with action type

4. **Set state: requesting_<action>**
   - Lifecycle manager checks this before acting

5. **Wait for termination**
   - Don't self-exit - let manager kill session
   - Ensures clean handoff

## Lifecycle Request Flow

Agent                           Lifecycle Manager
  |                                    |
  | 1. gt handoff --cycle              |
  |    a. Verify git clean             |
  |    b. Send handoff mail to self    |
  |    c. Set requesting_cycle=true    |
  |    d. Send lifecycle request       |
  |------------------------------------→|
  |                                    |
  |                   2. Receive request
  |                   3. Verify state  |
  |                   4. Kill session  |
  |                   5. Start new     |
  |                      (for cycle)   |
  |                                    |
  |         New session reads handoff  |
  |         Resumes work               |

## Who Manages Whom

| Agent | Sends lifecycle request to |
|-------|---------------------------|
| Polecat | <rig>/witness |
| Refinery | <rig>/witness |
| Witness | daemon/ |
| Mayor | daemon/ |

## Implementation

1. Detect current role (polecat, witness, refinery, mayor, crew)
2. Apply context-aware default if no flag specified
3. Run pre-flight checks (git clean, work handed off)
4. Send handoff mail to self (if cycling)
5. Send lifecycle request to appropriate manager
6. Set requesting_<action> in state.json
7. Wait (manager will kill us)

## For Polecats (--shutdown)

Additional cleanup after kill:
- Witness removes worktree
- Witness deletes polecat branch
- Polecat ceases to exist

## Related Issues

- gt-99m: Daemon (handles Mayor/Witness lifecycle)
- gt-7ik: Ephemeral polecats (polecat cleanup)
- gt-eu9: Witness session cycling","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-18T11:39:40.806863-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T18:18:22.35369-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-gby","depends_on_id":"gt-7ik","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T11:39:46.423945-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-gby","depends_on_id":"gt-eu9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T11:39:46.547204-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-gby","depends_on_id":"gt-99m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T11:50:24.142182-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-gl2","title":"Clarify Mayor vs Witness cleanup responsibilities","description":"Document the cleanup authority model: Witness owns ALL per-worker cleanup, Mayor never involved.

## The Rule

**Witness handles ALL per-worker cleanup. Mayor is never involved.**

## Why This Matters

1. Separation of concerns: Mayor strategic, Witness operational
2. Reduced coordination overhead: No back-and-forth for routine cleanup
3. Faster shutdown: Witness kills workers immediately upon verification
4. Cleaner escalation: Mayor only hears about problems

## What Witness Handles

- Verifying worker git state before kill
- Nudging workers to fix dirty state
- Killing worker sessions
- Updating worker state (sleep/wake)
- Logging verification results

## What Mayor Handles

- Receiving swarm complete notifications
- Deciding whether to start new swarms
- Handling escalations (stuck workers after 3 retries)
- Cross-rig coordination

## Escalation Path

Worker stuck -> Witness nudges (up to 3x) -> Witness escalates to Mayor -> Mayor decides: force kill, reassign, or human

## Anti-Patterns

DO NOT: Mayor asks Witness if worker X is clean
DO: Witness reports swarm complete, all workers verified

DO NOT: Mayor kills worker sessions directly
DO: Mayor tells Witness to abort swarm, Witness handles cleanup

DO NOT: Workers report done to Mayor
DO: Workers report to Witness, Witness aggregates and reports up","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T19:48:56.678724-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T20:48:12.068964-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-gl2","depends_on_id":"gt-82y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T19:49:05.929877-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-h262","title":"bd ready --blockers: prioritize issues that block other work","description":"bd ready should prioritize issues that are blocking other work.

**From VC**: Blocker-first prioritization in GetReadyWork(). ~100 lines.
Algorithm: baseline-failure first, then discovered:blocker, then by priority.

**Gas Town implementation**: CLI flag or default behavior:
```bash
bd ready --blockers-first  # Or make this default
```

Checks dependency graph. Issues with many dependents surface first.

**Value**: Unblocks parallelism faster. Critical path gets cleared.

-{"id":"gt-h28m","title":"Deacon patrol banners: visual feedback on atom transitions","description":"Print large ASCII banners when transitioning between patrol atoms.

## Problem

When the deacon progresses through patrol atoms (steps), there is no visual feedback.
The operator cannot easily see what the deacon is doing without reading the full output.

## Desired Behavior

Print banners on step start and completion:

  INBOX-CHECK - Checking for lifecycle requests, escalations, timers
  INBOX-CHECK COMPLETE - Processed 3 messages, 0 lifecycle requests

## Benefits

1. Scanability: Operator can glance at tmux and see what is happening
2. Progress tracking: Easy to see where in the patrol loop we are
3. Debugging: Clear demarcation between steps for troubleshooting

## Implementation Options

1. In deacon CLAUDE.md: Instruct agent to print banners
2. gt patrol step start/end: Commands that print banners
3. bd mol step hooks: Automatically on step transitions

## Related

- gt-id36: Deacon Kernel
- gt-rana: Patrol System","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-22T03:04:14.290474-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:54:55.972256-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T14:54:55.972256-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented in deacon.md.tmpl - startup banner, step banners with emojis, cycle summary banner. -{"id":"gt-lek6","title":"gt rig reset --stale: Clear orphaned in_progress items","description":"Reset in_progress issues when their assigned agent no longer exists.

## Problem
When polecats die without cleanup, their issues remain in_progress forever.
Need a way to bulk-reset these orphaned items.

## Command
```bash
gt rig reset --stale [--dry-run]
```

## Logic
For each in_progress issue in rig:
1. Parse assignee (e.g., "gastown/furiosa")
2. Map to tmux session name (gt-gastown-furiosa)
3. If session does NOT exist:
   - Reset status to "open"
   - Clear assignee
4. Exception: skip crew/* assignees (persistent identities)
   OR check if crew tmux session exists

## Output
```
Resetting stale work in gastown:
  gt-abc: gastown/furiosa (no session) → open
  gt-def: gastown/nux (no session) → open
  Skipped: gt-xyz: gastown/crew/max (persistent)
Reset 2 issues, skipped 1
```

## Related
- gt-2kz: CLI cleanup commands for stale state
- gt-rdmw: orphan-check in deacon patrol
- gt-orphans command (list orphaned molecules)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-21T21:33:46.962413-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T15:16:22.295127-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T15:16:22.295127-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented --stale flag for gt rig reset with dry-run support"}

4. Exception: skip crew/* assignees (persistent identities)

 {"id":"gt-lno","title":"Swarm state persistence: manifest + state + events","description":"Upgrade swarm persistence to match PGT pattern.

## Current State
Single .gastown/swarms.json with all swarms.

## Target State (per-swarm)
```
.gastown/swarms/
└── <swarm-id>/
    ├── manifest.json  # Immutable config
    ├── state.json     # Mutable progress
    ├── events.jsonl   # Audit log
    └── report.md      # Generated report
```

## File Schemas

### manifest.json (immutable after creation)
```json
{
  "id": "sw-1",
  "title": "Epic description",
  "epic_id": "gt-abc",
  "rig": "gastown",
  "base_commit": "abc123",
  "integration_branch": "swarm/sw-1",
  "target_branch": "main",
  "workers": ["Toast", "Nux"],
  "tasks": [{"id": "gt-xyz", "title": "..."}],
  "created_at": "2024-01-01T00:00:00Z"
}
```

### state.json (mutable)
```json
{
  "state": "active",
  "task_states": {
    "gt-xyz": {"state": "merged", "assignee": "Toast"}
  },
  "updated_at": "2024-01-01T01:00:00Z",
  "error": null
}
```

### events.jsonl (append-only audit)
```jsonl
{"event": "created", "ts": "...", "data": {...}}
{"event": "task_assigned", "ts": "...", "data": {...}}
{"event": "task_merged", "ts": "...", "data": {...}}
```

## Benefits
- Audit trail via events.jsonl
- Manifest immutability prevents corruption
- Cleaner separation of concerns
- Per-swarm isolation

## Migration
Keep backward compat with swarms.json during transition.

## Files to Modify
- internal/swarm/manager.go: Refactor persistence
- internal/cmd/swarm.go: SwarmStore → directory-based

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Per-swarm directory structure
- [ ] Events logged to JSONL
- [ ] Manifest immutable after creation
- [ ] List command aggregates from directories","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T14:48:14.151538-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T17:23:19.716405-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-16T17:23:19.716405-08:00"}
